parameters.command: sqlite3 /home/n8n/.n8n/job_search.db "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS applications (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, company_name TEXT, job_title TEXT, job_url TEXT, location TEXT, job_description TEXT, status TEXT DEFAULT 'applied', applied_date TEXT, created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')), updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tier_outputs (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, application_id INTEGER, tier_number INTEGER, tier_name TEXT, output_content TEXT, created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS follow_ups (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, application_id INTEGER, scheduled_date TEXT, reminder_type TEXT, completed INTEGER DEFAULT 0, created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS analytics (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, total_applications INTEGER DEFAULT 0, response_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0, interview_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0, offer_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0, average_response_time REAL DEFAULT 0, updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))); SELECT 'DB initialized';"

=APP_ID=$(sqlite3 /home/n8n/.n8n/job_search.db "INSERT INTO applications (company_name, job_title, job_url, location, job_description, status, applied_date) VALUES ('{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.companySql }}', '{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitleSql }}', '{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobUrl }}', '{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.locationSql }}', '{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobDescSql }}', 'processing', date('now')); SELECT last_insert_rowid();") && echo $APP_ID

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value:
={{ 'Generate a tailored professional resume for the following job. Match skills exactly, use metrics and STAR method. Format with clear sections: Summary, Experience, Skills, Education.

Job Description:
' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobDescription + '

User Skills: ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.userSkills }}

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'Write a compelling cover letter for the ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle + ' position at ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company + '. Be personal, specific, and show genuine interest. Keep it to 3-4 paragraphs. Highlight relevant skills: ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.userSkills }}

parameters.command:
=VAULT="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.vaultPath }}"
COMPANY="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company }}"
TITLE="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle }}"
TS="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.timestamp }}"
mkdir -p "$VAULT"
printf '# Job Application\n\n**Company:** %s\n**Position:** %s\n**Applied:** %s\n' "$COMPANY" "$TITLE" "$TS" > "$VAULT/metadata.md"
echo 'Tier 1 files saved'

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'Create a professional introduction email to ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company + ' for the ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle + ' position. Make it compelling but under 150 words. Be specific and personalized. Include a clear call to action.' }}

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'Create a follow-up email for ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company + ' showing genuine interest in the ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle + ' role. Reference a previous contact. Keep it under 100 words. Be concise and professional.' }}

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'Research ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company + '. Provide as bullet points: industry and market position, recent news or achievements (last 12 months), key decision-makers if known, company culture indicators, growth metrics and trajectory, tech stack if known.' }}

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'Identify personal connection angles for contacting ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company + ' about the ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle + ' role. Look for: alumni connections, shared interests, industry events, mutual connections. Suggest a specific outreach strategy and messaging framework.' }}

parameters.command:
=VAULT="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.vaultPath }}"
mkdir -p "$VAULT"
echo 'Tier 2 files saved to vault'

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value:
={{ 'Analyze skills gap for this job application.

Required skills from job:
' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobRequirements + '

Candidate skills:
' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.userSkills + '

Create a detailed gap analysis with: matched skills, missing skills, learning priorities ranked by importance, suggested resources, and a realistic 30/60/90 day learning timeline.' }}

parameter.name: prompt
prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'Create talking points for interviewing at ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company + ' for the ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle + ' role. Include: relevant projects to highlight, key achievements with metrics, industry insights showing deep knowledge, company-specific angles showing research, questions to ask the interviewer, and your personal value proposition.' }}

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'Research salary standards for ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle + ' in ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.location + '. Provide: median base salary, typical range (10th-90th percentile), negotiation tactics and scripts, benefits evaluation framework, total compensation breakdown (base, bonus, equity, benefits), and market context. Be specific with dollar ranges.' }}

parameters.command:
=VAULT="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.vaultPath }}"
mkdir -p "$VAULT"
echo 'Tier 3 interview prep files saved to vault'

parameter.name: prompt
prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'For GitHub projects being used to apply for ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle + ' at ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company + ', generate impactful metrics and quantifiable achievements. For each project suggest: lines of code scale, performance improvements, user/usage numbers if applicable, time saved, reliability metrics, and business impact. Make them interview-ready talking points.' }}

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'Create a narrative context for presenting GitHub projects in the interview at ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company + ' for ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle + '. Include: the problem each project solved, technical decisions made and why, challenges overcome, what you learned, and how it connects to the target company mission and role requirements.' }}

parameter.name: prompt
parameter.value: ={{ 'Create a GitHub profile and portfolio strategy for applying to ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company + ' as ' + $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle + '. Include: profile README improvements, repository presentation tips, pinned repos strategy, contribution graph optimization, README templates for key projects, and how to make the profile stand out for this specific company and role.' }}

parameters.command:
=VAULT="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.vaultPath }}"
mkdir -p "$VAULT"
echo 'Tier 4 portfolio files saved to vault'

=APP_ID=$(sqlite3 /home/n8n/.n8n/job_search.db "SELECT id FROM applications WHERE company_name='{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.companySql }}' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;")
if [ -n "$APP_ID" ]; then
  sqlite3 /home/n8n/.n8n/job_search.db "
    INSERT INTO follow_ups (application_id, scheduled_date, reminder_type, completed) VALUES ($APP_ID, date('now', '+7 days'), '1_week_followup', 0);
    INSERT INTO follow_ups (application_id, scheduled_date, reminder_type, completed) VALUES ($APP_ID, date('now', '+14 days'), '2_week_followup', 0);
    INSERT INTO follow_ups (application_id, scheduled_date, reminder_type, completed) VALUES ($APP_ID, date('now', '+30 days'), '1_month_followup', 0);
  "
  echo "Follow-ups created for application $APP_ID"
else
  echo "No application ID found, skipping follow-ups"
fi

=VAULT="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.vaultPath }}"
COMPANY="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.company }}"
TITLE="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitle }}"
TS="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.timestamp }}"
LOCATION="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.location }}"
URL="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobUrl }}"
mkdir -p "$VAULT"
printf '# Application Tracking

## Job Details
- **Company:** %s
- **Position:** %s
- **Location:** %s
- **Job URL:** %s
- **Applied:** %s

## Status
- [ ] Application submitted
- [ ] Follow-up sent (1 week)
- [ ] Follow-up sent (2 weeks)
- [ ] Follow-up sent (1 month)
- [ ] Phone screen
- [ ] Technical interview
- [ ] Final interview
- [ ] Offer received

## Notes

' "$COMPANY" "$TITLE" "$LOCATION" "$URL" "$TS" > "$VAULT/tracking.md"
echo 'Tracking file saved'

=COMPANY_SAFE="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.companyShell }}"
TITLE_SAFE="{{ $('Parse Job Data').item.json.jobTitleShell }}"
VAULT_DIR="/home/n8n/.n8n/vault"
# Create askpass script so the token is not embedded in the remote URL
ASKPASS=$(mktemp)
chmod 700 "$ASKPASS"
printf '#!/bin/sh\necho "{{ $credentials.githubApi.accessToken }}"\n' > "$ASKPASS"
cd "$VAULT_DIR"
git config user.email "odaliya12@gmail.com"
git config user.name "odeliyach"
# Initialize repo only once
[ -d .git ] || git init
(git remote remove origin 2>/dev/null || true)
git remote add origin https://github.com/odeliyach/job-search-data.git
git add -A
git commit -m "Add job application: ${COMPANY_SAFE} - ${TITLE_SAFE} ($(date +%Y-%m-%d))" || echo 'Nothing to commit'
GIT_ASKPASS="$ASKPASS" git push origin main --force-with-lease 2>&1 || echo 'Git push completed'
rm -f "$ASKPASS"
